documents: As of 2025, there are 563,947 people residing in Genoa, of whom 48.1% were male and 51.9% were female.

Genoa has the lowest birth rate and is the most aged of any large Italian city. Minors (children ages 18 and younger) totalled only 14.12% of the population compared to pensioners who number 26.67%. This compares with the Italian average of 18.06% for minors and 19.94% for pensioners. The median age of Genoa's residents is 47, compared to the Italian average of 42.
The current birth rate of the city is only 7.49 births per 1,000 inhabitants, compared to the national average of 9.45.
The Genoa metropolitan area had a GDP amounting to $30.1 billion in 2011, or $33,003 per capita.
user_query: Are there more males than females in Genoa? And what is the distribution?
